hints.text: Plates **fully white, black text** (Europe usu. has a blue strip)

shared_with.note: Kazakh plates are also mostly white — with only a **small blue hint**.
shared_with.diff: Camera: KZ = **white pickup** (often Gen4); RU = black/white car w/ long antenna or Gen4.

shared_with.note: Also ex-USSR, but Kyrgyz plates carry a **red strip**.
shared_with.diff: Red strip on the plate → Kyrgyzstan.

hints.text: Russian **Cyrillic** with the special letters **Ы Э Ё Ъ**
bullets: **Ё** also exists in Mongolian, **Ъ** in Bulgarian
Russian appears rarely in **Ukraine** and **eastern Estonia**

shared_with.note: You may rarely come across Russian text in Ukraine.
shared_with.diff: Ukrainian letters **Є Ґ І Ї** (Latin-looking і) + plate with blue-yellow strip → Ukraine.

shared_with.note: Bulgarian shares **Ъ** (used as a vowel mid-word).
shared_with.diff: Cyrillic+**Latin mix** on signs + EU blue plate strip → Bulgaria.

shared_with.note: Mongolian Cyrillic shares **Ё** and adds **Ө Ү** + double vowels (аа, ээ).
shared_with.diff: Double vowels, **ЗОГС** stop signs, yurts → Mongolia.

shared_with.note: Kazakh signage is often monolingual Russian.
shared_with.diff: Kazakh extras **Ә Ғ Қ Ң Ө Ұ Ү Һ І** anywhere → Kazakhstan.

shared_with.note: Kyrgyz is Cyrillic too.
shared_with.diff: Extra letters **Ө Ү Ң** + silver Gen3 car w/ 4 bars → Kyrgyzstan.

shared_with.note: Russian appears in **eastern Estonia** (Narva area).
shared_with.diff: Estonian (Latin, õäöü) dominates elsewhere; EU plates → Estonia.

hints.text: Key words: street = **улица** (ул.), city = **город** (г.)
bullets: Bulgarian & most Slavic languages also use улица/ulica
Ukrainian differs: street = **вулиця** (вул.), city = **місто** (м.)

shared_with.note: Nearly identical signage style.
shared_with.diff: **вул.** instead of ул. and **м.** instead of г. → Ukraine.

shared_with.note: улица is used in Bulgarian too (abbreviated ul.).
shared_with.diff: Latin transliteration next to Cyrillic + EU plates → Bulgaria.

hints.text: Three main bollard types: very **thin on a stick**, **black top + red vertical reflector**, and a German-style one

shared_with.note: One Russian type IS the German-style bollard.
shared_with.diff: Latin script, EU plates, German road furniture → Germany.

hints.text: Near intersections: a very **large number of bollards**; intersections unusually **wide**
hints.uniq_note: Bollards and wide roads exist elsewhere (esp. Kazakhstan/Ukraine with similar post-Soviet layouts) — but the sheer bollard **quantity** at huge intersections is a strong Russia tell. Confirm with plates & language.

shared_with.note: Kazakhstan's infrastructure (poles, signs, bollards) is very Russia-like.
shared_with.diff: Cyrillic + Russian plates → Russia; Kazakh context (steppe, Kazakh script mixed in) → Kazakhstan.

shared_with.note: Ukraine shares the same post-Soviet wide-road, bollard-heavy layout in many regions.
shared_with.diff: No Ukrainian plates/language present → Russia.

hints.text: Signposts often have **black-painted bottoms**

shared_with.note: Black signpost bottoms are also common in Kazakhstan.
shared_with.diff: White pickup camera + Kazakh letters → Kazakhstan.

hints.text: Mainly **square concrete poles**; often **concrete support blocks** at the pole base (wooden poles raised on the support)

shared_with.note: Square concrete poles, often with **diagonal support beams**.
shared_with.diff: Diagonal beams + вул. + blue-yellow plate strip → Ukraine.

shared_with.note: Post-Soviet square concrete poles too.
shared_with.diff: Latin script + EU plates → Lithuania.

shared_with.note: Post-Soviet square concrete poles too.
shared_with.diff: Estonian language (õäöü) + EU plates → Estonia.

shared_with.note: Very Russia-like poles & infrastructure.
shared_with.diff: White pickup camera car → Kazakhstan.

shared_with.note: Square concrete poles as well (also Georgia).
shared_with.diff: Red plate strip + silver 4-bar Gen3 car → Kyrgyzstan.

hints.text: Gen3: **black or white car with a long antenna** (sometimes short antenna without visible car)
bullets: Short antenna w/o car is common across Gen3 **Europe**; the long-antenna black/white cars are much rarer there

shared_with.note: Ukraine's 2011 coverage also has black/white cars with a long antenna.
shared_with.diff: вул., blue-yellow plate strip, worse roads → Ukraine.

hints.text: Pedestrian signs: simply drawn person, **3 stripes**, often a bright **yellow border**

shared_with.note: Same 3-stripe design.
shared_with.diff: вул. + plate strip → Ukraine.

shared_with.note: Same design.
shared_with.diff: Latin script + EU plates → Lithuania.

shared_with.note: Same design.
shared_with.diff: Double vowels, yurts, ЗОГС → Mongolia.

shared_with.note: Same design.
shared_with.diff: White pickup + Kazakh letters → Kazakhstan.

shared_with.note: Same design.
shared_with.diff: Red plate strip + Ө Ү Ң → Kyrgyzstan.

hints.text: Pedestrian crossings: alternating **white and yellow** stripes

shared_with.note: Also yellow+white crossings — but **with white dashes on either side**.
shared_with.diff: Dashes beside the crossing → Kazakhstan (Russia lacks them).

hints.text: **Black & white striped** guardrails, kerbs and bridge barriers
bullets: B&W striped **pole shields** are common along highways, esp. in European Russia

shared_with.note: B&W painted guardrails are a Ukraine staple too.
shared_with.diff: вул. + plate strip + rougher roads → Ukraine.

hints.text: Urban housing = large rectangular **concrete apartment blocks** (newer areas more modern)

shared_with.note: Soviet-era apartment blocks look near-identical.
shared_with.diff: Language details (вул., і/ї) and plates decide.

shared_with.note: Soviet blocks as well.
shared_with.diff: White pickup + Kazakh letters → Kazakhstan.

hints.text: Rural housing: basic **wooden houses with metal roofs**
Richly **decorated window frames** (often painted blue) on traditional homes/log cabins
Urban areas: **exposed above-ground pipes** held up by vertical pipes

shared_with.note: Above-ground pipes exist too (and in Georgia), but less commonly.
shared_with.diff: Frequency + language/plates decide.

shared_with.note: **Gas pipes everywhere** in Kazakh cities — even more than in Russia.
shared_with.diff: White pickup camera → Kazakhstan.

hints.text: **Birch trees** (white peeling bark) range over most of Russia — almost never far south or far north in the west
bullets: **Clustered birches** / pure birch forests → east of the Urals, mostly Chelyabinsk ↔ Novosibirsk

hints.text: **Siberian larches** (needle-like leaves) dominate much of the east — more prevalent the further east / higher up
**Spruce vs fir vs larch**: spruce = dense, droopy/parallel dark branches
bullets: **Caucasian fir** (Caucasus only): straighter, well-organised branches
**Larches**: upward, less dense, 'unruly/unhealthy' look — use with caution in eastern-looking rounds

hints.text: **Flower metas**: fireweed (pink) → north; sosnowsky hogweed (white) → Moscow/Komi/Perm; sunflowers → Ukraine border + Ulyanovsk/Samara/N-Orenburg; blue-pod lupines → N-Vladimir/E-Ivanovo/SW-Kostroma
**Tree metas near borders**: black poplar → south (Ukraine border) + west Kazakh border; Russian olive → Kazakhstan border
**Sandy roadsides** → Khanty-Mansi, Yamalo-Nenets, around Nizhny Novgorod (Volga), Karelia, Murmansk, W-Sakha (beware: also near rivers elsewhere)
**Red soil** → around Izhevsk & Perm, much of Arkhangelsk, Leningrad & Pskov Oblast, near Volgograd/Astrakhan (non-exhaustive — appears near water/iron mines anywhere)
**Far-north tundra**: relatively flat with very low vegetation
West of the Caspian Sea: extremely **dry & flat** (Kalmykia, Astrakhan, Dagestan)
**Caucasus** = one of Russia's largest ranges; Mt. **Elbrus** (highest) on the Kabardino-Balkaria / Karachay-Cherkessia border
**Rocks along the road** → most of Karelia & Murmansk (smaller/rarer in Perm Krai & Buryatia)
**RHD Japanese import cars** get commoner the further **east** you go (from ~Novosibirsk) — fast east/west tell
Very **thin stick-mounted bollards** → eastern half, starting around Omsk (normal bollards still plentiful)
**Black traffic-sign bottoms** → Stavropol, Chechnya, W-Dagestan (rare uncoloured exceptions in other southern subjects)
Roads made of **concrete blocks** → the north: Komi, Nenets, Yamalo-Nenets
Town names in **Cyrillic + Latin** → near the Baltic border & around Moscow (less often near Finland)
**Brown tourist signs** → Moscow Oblast + bordering oblasts (elsewhere only near attractions/city centres)
**Red-brick-only buildings** → mostly south (+ Magnitogorsk, Orsk, Omsk); more brick in view = more reliable
**Mosques** → Muslim-majority south + Tatarstan & Bashkortostan
**Full coverage map** — very sparse in the east & north; knowing the gaps helps
**Gen3 white car (long antenna)** hotspots: southern Chelyabinsk, snow coverage around Novosibirsk, Far East from Amur Oblast
bullets: Kamchatka's 'silver' car often looks white (B-type antenna) — the main exception to black/white

hints.text: **Short-antenna types** (Gen3) region-guess Russia
bullets: **A-type** (ridge right): Kaluga, Orenburg, Krasnodar
**B-type** (ridge left): Nizhny Novgorod, Elista, Yekaterinburg–Tyumen
**B pronounced** (rectangular ridge): Moscow Obl, Kurgan, Krasnoyarsk, SW-Krasnodar
**C-type** (ridges lower, 'twisting'): north & east of Moscow, down to Rostov
**Blurred**: two clusters — around Ufa and Stavropol→Makhachkala
**Tilted** (leans left): St. Petersburg → Volgograd diagonal (+Stavropol, Ufa)

hints.text: **Long-antenna types** (Gen3)
bullets: **A-type** (ridge right): Leningrad Obl, Karelia, Vologda — almost all WHITE-car long antenna is A-type
**B-type** (ridge left): Yaroslavl→Vladimir, Tula, much near Tuva & Irkutsk
**Blurred**: Sakhalin & eastern roads, much of Khanty-Mansi (+far-west Bryansk, some Tula)

hints.text: **Gen4 seasons** region-guess
bullets: **Winter**: south — Ryazan down to Kabardino-Balkaria & Dagestan; heavy Gen4 snow → Saratov + E-Voronezh
**Spring**: Kemerovo/Krasnoyarsk, Orenburg/Kamyshin, Lipetsk, Moscow city
**Summer**: the default nearly everywhere
**Autumn**: rare — Kazan, few Murmansk roads, some Ulan-Ude
Altai mountains & most of the Far East have **no Gen4** at all

hints.text: **Gen4 car colours**
bullets: **Red** (most common): Khanty-Mansi, highways down to Irkutsk/Ulan-Ude, Penza/Tatarstan, Rostov-on-Don
**White**: May coverage near Lipetsk, Moscow city, all coverage north of Berkakit (Sakha) + Magadan
**Black**: Moscow, Tver, Voronezh (+some Tatarstan & south)

hints.text: Area codes — the leading digit gives the zone:
bullets: **3-** = central
**4-** = east or around Moscow (401 Kaliningrad, ~410–430 far east, 470+ Moscow region; Moscow city = 495/499)

hints.text: Gen3 sometimes has **unblurred plates** — regional code on the right; ordered alphabetically within subject type (01 = Adygea, 28 = Amur; 3-digit: drop first digit, 123 → 23 Krasnodar)
**Bus stops** styles are unique to specific federal subjects — memorable: Krasnoyarsk Krai, Chuvashia, Tatarstan, Mari El
**Bilingual minority-language signs** (town entries, street names)
bullets: **Tatarstan**: Tatar 'урамы' (abbr. ур.) instead of улица
**Bashkortostan**: Bashkir 'урамы', usually not abbreviated
**Chuvashia**: 'урамӗ'
**Mari El**: 'урем'
**Komi**: 'улича'

hints.text: **Lined oak trees** (often w/ a single white trunk stripe) are extremely common
Big **red brake light** in view; Gen3 here is **autumn-only** (autumn Gen3 otherwise: Bryansk, Tula, Belgorod); cobblestones common
Summer **Gen4**: green fields interrupted by isolated **birch patches** — spills into S-Tyumen & Novosibirsk (near Tatarsk); in Gen3 this look covers a much larger area
**Sparsely planted roadside larches** + open rolling fields (also adjacent subjects); **yellow pole plates** on most poles
Grassy fields + bushy vegetation in **early-spring Gen4** (flat or mountainous); typical flowers; **white limestone brick** buildings (also adjacent regions)
**Giant butterbur** ('Sakhalin cabbage') very common, esp. southern half (related plants very rarely in Adygea); blurred long antenna
Unique **grey gravelly soil** + the most **volcanoes** of any region (close-up e.g. in Klyuchi); most Russian **trekker coverage** is Kamchatka

Gravelly/dark soil toward the mountains **east of Yakutsk** (from ~Keskil); main road **south of Yakutsk**: larches + big mountains, Gen4 **white car north of Berkakit**; very **hazy** coverage east/south of Mirny (often orange/white haze, Vilyuy river)
Noticeably warmer: **lush, diverse hilly forests**; mostly overcast w/ white long-antenna car; open rolling hills near the tri-border
Eastern half of the **Kolyma Highway**: tall hazy hills/mountains, unpaved dark gravel (haze absent in the western half)
Open grassy landscape w/ shrubs + mountains (lusher near Baikal); **forest-fire haze** near Lake Gusinoye (vs Mirny haze: Buryatia = open rolling land)
Main road: **black car, long antenna, driving north** through the Altai mountains — often foggy/rainy
**Dry steppe mountains + long antenna** → Tuva; south Altai road looks similar — use driving direction & clouds; also has Kursk-style outer dashed lines
Open hilly land, **low shrubbery + fireweed** (lower shrubs = further north); Gen4 main road & some 2018 Karelia roads: **two yellow bands** on most signposts
**Outer dashed road lines** throughout Kursk Oblast (exceptions: Tuva; rarely Karelia, Primorsky, Khabarovsk)
**Clips on the edges of signposts** (very rarely Pskov/Novgorod or elsewhere)
**Extended insulator bars** on poles (also S-Kemerovo); **rounded rectangular fences with a middle line**
Bollard with a **grey rectangle + double red reflectors** — specific to the **P100**
Distinct **decorative pedestrian-sign holders**
Historic buildings: **green roofs, red brick + white stone details** (churches similar)
**Blue tourist signs with English translations** — only in Moscow
Street signs with a **transliteration on black background** (mainly downtown)
Exclusive **circular street signs** (up to 4 languages); striped **red-white poles** (also a few other Mordovia cities)
**Alternating red-and-white poles** (less commonly greater Nizhny Novgorod, randomly elsewhere)
Utility poles with a **mesh framework** (much rarer in e.g. Tolyatti)
**Red-painted poles** — only in Murom
**Angled extended insulator bar** on poles
Pedestrian signs on a **U-shaped hook**
Unique **hanging pedestrian signs**
Fence with a **thin rounded-rectangle top** (Tolyatti often paints it yellow)
Decorative fences, usually **bright orange**
**Far-east / north spotlights**
bullets: **Vorkuta** (N-Komi): dead-looking winter, sometimes reddish soil, big apartment-block foundations
**Sovetskaya Gavan & Vanino** (E-Khabarovsk): similar winter — but Vorkuta is flatter/more barren
**Road to Okha** (N-Sakhalin): unpaved, very sandy, stunted bushes
**Khabarovsk–Vanino road**: sunny mountains, drives west, black car w/ blurred long antenna
**Jewish AO**: one covered road — rainy/foggy west side, wet-after-rain east side
**P-297 (Amur)**: sunset sun-on-horizon between Never & Sivaki

hints.text: **Baikal & south spotlights**
bullets: **Olkhon Island** (Baikal): single road, rolling sandy hills at sunset, lake + ridge across the bay
**Tunkinsky NP** (W-Buryatia): green river basin, tall jagged mountains north — much greener than the rest of Buryatia
**N-Dagestan / SW-Astrakhan**: flat rural Gen4 on poorly-tracked dirt roads (unique in that desert region)
**Sochi / Black Sea**: the only place with **palm trees** / tropical plants
